Since the length is twice the width, I can let the width be something, say, 'w', then the length would be 2w. That makes sense. So substituting into the perimeter formula, it would be 2*(2w + w) = 2*(3w) = 6w. Wait, so the perimeter is 6w, and they told us the perimeter is 54 cm. Therefore, 6w = 54. Wait, let me double-check. If the sides are 18 cm and 9 cm, then multiplying them gives 162. And the perimeter is 2*(18+9) = 2*27 = 54 cm. Yep, that matches. So the area is 162 cm².
